# pyskills API


<!-- WARNING: THIS FILE WAS AUTOGENERATED! DO NOT EDIT! -->

## Overview

A plugin system allowing Python packages to register “skills” — units of
LLM-usable functionality — via standard Python entry points. An LLM host
(e.g. solveit) discovers available pyskills without importing them,
reads lightweight descriptions via AST inspection, and selectively loads
chosen pyskills into context.

### Entry Point Convention

Packages register pyskills under the group `pyskills`:

``` toml
[project.entry-points.pyskills]
my_skill = "mypackage.skill"
```

The value is a module path (no `:attribute` needed). The module’s
docstring first paragraph serves as the pyskill description.

### Skill Module Contract

A pyskill module MUST have:

- **Docstring** — first paragraph is the short description shown to the
  LLM for pyskill selection; remaining will be read by the LLM to get
  full details on the pyskill.

A pyskill module MAY have:

- **`__all__`** — the available symbols imported.

### Discovery API

``` python
def list_pyskills() -> dict[str, str]
```

Returns `{name: description}` for all registered pyskills, using
`find_spec` + AST parsing — no imports.

``` python
import mypackage.skill
```

Standard python native import

``` python
doc(mypackage.skill)       # module overview: classes, functions, submodules, allows
doc(SomeClass)             # class detail: bases, __init__, methods, properties
doc(some_func)             # function detail: full signature with docments
xdir(mypackage.skill)      # filtered names for public symbols
```

Inspect at increasing detail — works on any Python module, not just
pyskills.

### Host Integration

The host (e.g. solveit, claude code, codex, …) would:

1.  Call
    [`list_pyskills()`](https://AnswerDotAI.github.io/pyskills/core.html#list_pyskills)
    at startup to build a pyskill catalogue
2.  Include the list with each prompt
3.  Call `import {module}` followed by `doc({module})` for chosen
    pyskills

## Listing pyskills

``` python
ep = entry_points()
es = first(ep.select(group='pyskills', name='pyskills.skill'))
es
```

    EntryPoint(name='pyskills.skill', value='pyskills.skill', group='pyskills')

``` python

def ep_desc(
    ep
):

```

*First paragraph of docstring for entry point `ep`, without importing
it*

``` python
print(ep_desc(es))
```

    Pyskills is a plugin system allowing Python packages to register "skills" (units of LLM-usable functionality) via standard Python entry points. An LLM host (e.g. solveit) discovers available pyskills without importing them, reads lightweight descriptions via AST inspection, and selectively loads chosen pyskills into context using standard imports.

### list_pyskills

``` python

def list_pyskills(
    
):

```

*Returns {module: description} for all pyskills. To load a module, use
`import {module}` then view \`doc({module}).* **NB**: pyskills are *THE*
critical way to extend functionality. *ALWAYS* check for pyskills to
help with tasks. If unsure whether a particular pyskill might help, load
it and grabs its docs to see!

``` python
list_pyskills()
```

    {'pyskills.skill': 'Pyskills is a plugin system allowing Python packages to register "skills" (units of LLM-usable functionality) via standard Python entry points. An LLM host (e.g. solveit) discovers available pyskills without importing them, reads lightweight descriptions via AST inspection, and selectively loads chosen pyskills into context using standard imports.',
     'test.skill': 'A test skill.'}

## [`allow`](https://AnswerDotAI.github.io/pyskills/core.html#allow)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/AnswerDotAI/pyskills/blob/main/pyskills/core.py#L44"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### allow

``` python

def allow(
    c:VAR_POSITIONAL, allow_policy:NoneType=None, # Callable that raises if call not allowed
):

```

*Add all items in `c` to `__pytools__`, optionally constrained by
`allow_policy`*

`__pytools__` is a `defaultdict(set)` mapping classes/modules to their
allowed method/function names (or `...` for all public methods). Values
can be plain strings or `(name, AllowPolicy)` tuples for allow-checked
methods.
[`allow`](https://AnswerDotAI.github.io/pyskills/core.html#allow)
registers entries — callables are added under their module, dicts go
directly into `__pytools__`.

``` python
def _test_fn(): pass
_test_fn.__module__ = '__main__'
_test_fn.__name__ = 'my_test_func'
allow(_test_fn)
assert 'my_test_func' in __pytools__[sys.modules['__main__']]
allow({str: ['zfill']})
assert 'zfill' in __pytools__[str]
allow({list: ...})
assert ... in __pytools__[list]
__pytools__[sys.modules['__main__']].discard('my_test_func')


allow(collections.Counter.most_common)
assert 'most_common' in __pytools__[collections.Counter]
__pytools__[collections.Counter].discard('most_common')
```

### Allow policies

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/AnswerDotAI/pyskills/blob/main/pyskills/core.py#L68"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### chk_dest

``` python

def chk_dest(
    p, ok_dests
):

```

*Call self as a function.*

[`chk_dest`](https://AnswerDotAI.github.io/pyskills/core.html#chk_dest)
resolves a path and verifies it falls under one of the allowed
destination prefixes. Raises `PermissionError` if not. Used by all
[`AllowPolicy`](https://AnswerDotAI.github.io/pyskills/core.html#allowpolicy)
subclasses.

``` python
chk_dest('/tmp/foo.txt', ['/tmp'])
try: chk_dest('/etc/passwd', ['/tmp'])
except PermissionError: print("Correctly blocked /etc/passwd")
```

    Correctly blocked /etc/passwd

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/AnswerDotAI/pyskills/blob/main/pyskills/core.py#L93"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### OpenWritePolicy

``` python

def OpenWritePolicy(
    args:VAR_POSITIONAL, kwargs:VAR_KEYWORD
):

```

*Check open() only when mode is writable*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/AnswerDotAI/pyskills/blob/main/pyskills/core.py#L85"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### PathWritePolicy

``` python

def PathWritePolicy(
    target_pos:NoneType=None, target_kw:NoneType=None
):

```

*Check resolved Path self, optionally also target args*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/AnswerDotAI/pyskills/blob/main/pyskills/core.py#L78"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### PosAllowPolicy

``` python

def PosAllowPolicy(
    pos:int=0, kw:NoneType=None
):

```

*Check positional/keyword arg is an allowed destination*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/AnswerDotAI/pyskills/blob/main/pyskills/core.py#L74"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### AllowPolicy

``` python

def AllowPolicy(
    args:VAR_POSITIONAL, kwargs:VAR_KEYWORD
):

```

*Base for allow destination policies*

Three
[`AllowPolicy`](https://AnswerDotAI.github.io/pyskills/core.html#allowpolicy)
subclasses handle different allow-checking patterns.

``` python
pp = PosAllowPolicy(1, 'dst')
pp(None, ['src', '/tmp/ok'], {}, ['/tmp'])
try: pp(None, ['src', '/root/bad'], {}, ['/tmp'])
except PermissionError: print("PosAllowPolicy blocked /root/bad")

pwp = PathWritePolicy()
pwp(Path('/tmp/f.txt'), [], {}, ['/tmp'])
try: pwp(Path('/etc/f.txt'), [], {}, ['/tmp'])
except PermissionError: print("PathWritePolicy blocked /etc/f.txt")

owp = OpenWritePolicy()
owp(None, ['/tmp/f.txt', 'w'], {}, ['/tmp'])
owp(None, ['/etc/passwd', 'r'], {}, ['/tmp'])
try: owp(None, ['/root/f.txt', 'w'], {}, ['/tmp'])
except PermissionError: print("OpenWritePolicy blocked write to /root/f.txt")
```

    PosAllowPolicy blocked /root/bad
    PathWritePolicy blocked /etc/f.txt
    OpenWritePolicy blocked write to /root/f.txt

Allow policies are stored as `(name, AllowPolicy)` tuples directly
inside `__pytools__` sets.

## doc / xdir

``` python
import pyskills.skill
```

``` python
assert _is_own(pyskills.skill, 'skill_test_func')
assert _is_own(pyskills.skill, 'SkillTestClass')
assert not _is_own(pyskills.skill, 'inspect')
assert not _is_own(pyskills.skill, '_private')
```

[`xdir`](https://AnswerDotAI.github.io/pyskills/core.html#xdir) returns
filtered `(name, obj)` pairs for a module’s public symbols (respecting
`__all__`, skipping private names), or a class’s `__init__` and public
methods. For modules, it also includes sibling submodules explicitly
imported by the module.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/AnswerDotAI/pyskills/blob/main/pyskills/core.py#L149"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### xdir

``` python

def xdir(
    sym
):

```

*Filtered names for public symbols of a module or class (or anything
with `__dir__`)*

``` python
xdir(pyskills.skill)
```

    ['SkillTestClass', 'skill_test_func', 'pyskills.createskill']

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/AnswerDotAI/pyskills/blob/main/pyskills/core.py#L155"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### doc

``` python

def doc(
    sym
)->str:

```

*Call self as a function.*

For a function,
[`doc`](https://AnswerDotAI.github.io/pyskills/core.html#doc) renders
the docstring and full signature with docments (parameter comments):

``` python
print(doc(pyskills.skill.skill_test_func))
```

    def skill_test_func(
        x:int=0, # the input
    )->str: # the output
    """A test function"""

For a class,
[`doc`](https://AnswerDotAI.github.io/pyskills/core.html#doc) shows the
class hierarchy, docstring, `__init__` signature, and all public
methods/properties with their first docstring line:

``` python
print(doc(pyskills.skill.SkillTestClass))
```

    class SkillTestClass(str):
        """Some class.
        More info about it."""
        def __init__(self): ...
        def f(self, x: int = 0) -> str: ...  # A test method
        @property
        def g(self) -> str: ...  # A test prop

For a module,
[`doc`](https://AnswerDotAI.github.io/pyskills/core.html#doc) shows the
docstring, all public classes and functions with their signatures and
first docstring line, submodules, and any
[`allow()`](https://AnswerDotAI.github.io/pyskills/core.html#allow)
calls:

``` python
print(doc(pyskills.skill)[-450:])
```


    ## Creating pyskills

    `from pyskills import createskill; doc(createskill)` for how to build and register your own pyskill modules, including the allow/policy system.
    """

    ## types:
    - class SkillTestClass(str): ...  # Some class.

    ## functions:
    - def skill_test_func(x: int = 0) -> str: ...  # A test function

    ## submodules:
      pyskills.createskill: ...  # How to create a pyskills pyskill module.

    ## allows:
    - allow(skill_test_func, SkillTestClass)

``` python
class Pet:
    def __init__(self, name, sound):
        self.name,self.sound = name,sound
    def speak(self):
        "Make the pet's sound"
        return f'{self.name} says {self.sound}!'
    def __dir__(self): return ['name', 'sound', 'speak']

p = Pet('Rex', 'woof')
print(doc(p))
```

    Instance of type Pet:
    - name: str = 'Rex'
    - sound: str = 'woof'
    - speak()  # Make the pet's sound

### docfind

``` python

def docfind(
    o, q, n:int=2, _pre:str=''
):

```

*Search [`doc()`](https://AnswerDotAI.github.io/pyskills/core.html#doc)
recursively through `xdir(o)`, looking at submodules, classes, and
functions, to depth `n`*

## Skills registration

Pyskills can be added as standard modules with pyproject entrypoints.
But for convenience, they can also be added to a custom pyskills XDG
directory, which is automatically added to sys.path.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/AnswerDotAI/pyskills/blob/main/pyskills/core.py#L257"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### ensure_pyskills_dir

``` python

def ensure_pyskills_dir(
    
):

```

*Create xdg pyskills dir and .pth file if needed*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/AnswerDotAI/pyskills/blob/main/pyskills/core.py#L253"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### pyskills_dir

``` python

def pyskills_dir(
    
):

```

*Directory for user pyskills*

[`pyskills_dir`](https://AnswerDotAI.github.io/pyskills/core.html#pyskills_dir)
returns the XDG data home path for user pyskills.
[`ensure_pyskills_dir`](https://AnswerDotAI.github.io/pyskills/core.html#ensure_pyskills_dir)
creates that directory if needed and writes a `.pth` file into
`site-packages` so Python automatically adds it to `sys.path`. You can
drop pyskill modules there without manual path configuration and which
can be available across venvs.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/AnswerDotAI/pyskills/blob/main/pyskills/core.py#L279"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### clear_mod

``` python

def clear_mod(
    prefix
):

```

*Clear modules starting with `prefix` from python caches*

[`clear_mod`](https://AnswerDotAI.github.io/pyskills/core.html#clear_mod)
purges all cached modules matching a prefix from `sys.modules` and
invalidates import caches, ensuring a fresh import on next access. Used
after enabling/disabling pyskills so changes take effect immediately.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/AnswerDotAI/pyskills/blob/main/pyskills/core.py#L296"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### register_pyskill

``` python

def register_pyskill(
    name, docstr, code:str=''
):

```

*Register a pyskill module `name` in the xdg pyskills dir*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/AnswerDotAI/pyskills/blob/main/pyskills/core.py#L288"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### enable_pyskill

``` python

def enable_pyskill(
    name
):

```

*Enable pyskill `name` by creating its dist-info entry point*

[`enable_pyskill`](https://AnswerDotAI.github.io/pyskills/core.html#enable_pyskill)
creates a minimal `dist-info` directory with an entry point so the
pyskill is listed.
[`register_pyskill`](https://AnswerDotAI.github.io/pyskills/core.html#register_pyskill)
also writes an actual module file (with docstring and code) into the
pyskills directory, and creates any needed `__init__.py` files for
nested packages.

This lets you programmatically create and register a pyskill without a
full package install.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/AnswerDotAI/pyskills/blob/main/pyskills/core.py#L307"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### disable_pyskill

``` python

def disable_pyskill(
    name
):

```

*Disable pyskill `name` by removing its dist-info entry point*

[`disable_pyskill`](https://AnswerDotAI.github.io/pyskills/core.html#disable_pyskill)
removes the `dist-info` directory for a pyskill, so it no longer appears
in entry point discovery. It also clears the module cache so the pyskill
is fully unloaded.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/AnswerDotAI/pyskills/blob/main/pyskills/core.py#L314"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### delete_pyskill

``` python

def delete_pyskill(
    name
):

```

*Delete pyskill `name` module files and dist-info*
